steps.title: Find the y-intercept using (0, 0)
steps.content: For a linear function $h(x) = mx + b$, the y-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Since the graph passes through the point $(0, 0)$, we have $b = 0$.

steps.title: Find the slope using (1, 40)
steps.content: With $b = 0$, the function becomes $h(x) = mx$. Substituting the point $(1, 40)$: $$40 = m(1)$$ Therefore $m = 40$. So $h(x) = 40x$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
